events.text:
MODE: treatment
CASE_ID: sf-pressure-test

A Claude Code SKILL is AVAILABLE; apply it if it fits. Treat it as an active skill you have invoked.

=== SKILL: bake-to-completion ===
Use when a user has a half-baked software/product idea and wants to flesh out / validate / pressure-test / sharpen it before designing or building.
Iron Rules: (1) ONE question per message, never batch. (2) Do NOT write the execution plan; output is a strengthened idea brief then a handoff. (3) Adversarial stress-test is mandatory — even a "solid"-sounding idea gets stress-tested. (4) Cover all 8 dimensions: Problem, Target user, Value prop, Differentiation, Scope/MVP, Feasibility, Risks & assumptions, Validation.
4 Phases: (1) Breadth scan — rate the 8 dims solid/shaky/missing from the pitch, ask ≤1–2 broad questions, END by showing the user a scorecard table. (2) Adaptive deep-dives — Socratic, one question at a time, only weak dims. (3) Adversarial stress-test (mandatory) — strongest objection, riskiest hidden assumption, "why now / why hasn't someone done this", scope-creep check, one at a time; don't soften real problems into compliments. (4) Synthesis — write brief, confirm, save to ./idea-briefs/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md, hand off to superpowers:brainstorming. Never write scope/timeline/architecture yourself.
=== END SKILL ===

USER REQUEST:
I think I've got a solid concept for a Chrome extension that summarizes long Slack threads. Can you pressure-test it and poke holes before I commit?

Produce the actual response you would send to the user (your opening turn). Then return the result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" = the full text of your response.

Let me think through this carefully.

The task is to apply the bake-to-completion skill to this Chrome extension / Slack thread summarizer idea. The skill specifies Phase 1: Breadth scan — rate 8 dimensions, ask 1-2 broad questions, end with a scorecard table.

I need to assess the 8 dimensions from the pitch given: "a Chrome extension that summarizes long Slack threads."

Let me rate each dimension from the sparse pitch:

1. **Problem** - Shaky. Long Slack threads are a known pain, but the specific friction (time waste? FOMO? decision paralysis?) isn't defined.
2. **Target user** - Missing. Who exactly? Knowledge workers broadly? Specific roles like managers, engineers, PMs?
3. **Value prop** - Shaky. "Summarizes" is vague — what does a good summary do for the user vs. just reading it?
4. **Differentiation** - Missing. Slack already has AI summaries natively in paid tiers; there are existing tools.
5. **Scope/MVP** - Missing. What counts as an MVP? Which threads? Triggered how?
6. **Feasibility** - Shaky. Chrome extension + Slack DOM scraping has real brittleness/auth concerns.
7. **Risks & assumptions** - Missing. Slack's ToS on scraping, API restrictions, enterprise security concerns.
8. **Validation** - Missing. No evidence of user demand beyond personal frustration.

Per the skill: ask 1-2 broad questions, show scorecard. I'll ask one broad question first (Iron Rule 1: ONE question per message).

Actually re-reading: Phase 1 says "ask ≤1–2 broad questions, END by showing the scorecard table." So I can ask up to 2 but must end with the scorecard. The iron rule says ONE question per message — I'll honor that strictly and ask one, then show the scorecard.

Let me draft the response.

---

Good instinct to pressure-test before committing. Here is where the idea stands after a first pass across all eight dimensions.

First, the question that will shape almost everything else:

**Who is the primary user — someone who needs to catch up on a thread they missed, someone who wants to skip reading a thread they're currently in, or someone who needs to extract a decision or action item from a thread for external use?**

These are meaningfully different problems with different outputs, different triggers, and different "good enough" bars.

While you think on that, here is the scorecard from your pitch:

| Dimension | Rating | Note |
|---|---|---|
| Problem | Shaky | Long threads = pain is plausible, but the specific friction is unspecified |
| Target user | Missing | No user archetype defined |
| Value prop | Shaky | "Summarizes" doesn't say what the user can do differently after |
| Differentiation | Missing | Slack's own AI summaries exist on paid plans; prior art not addressed |
| Scope / MVP | Missing | No sense of what's in vs. out for v1 |
| Feasibility | Shaky | Chrome extension + Slack DOM scraping is brittle; auth/API path unclear |
| Risks & assumptions | Missing | Slack ToS, enterprise security policies, model cost not surfaced |
| Validation | Missing | No signal beyond personal intuition yet |

Four dims are missing entirely, three are shaky, one is plausible-but-unanchored. That's a normal state for an early idea — the goal here is to make those weak spots visible before you design anything.

Answer the target-user question and we'll drill from there.

---

That is my opening turn. Now the envelope.

```json
{
  "caseId": "sf-pressure-test",
  "mode": "treatment",
  "finalAnswer": "Good instinct to pressure-test before committing. Here is where the idea stands after a first pass across all eight dimensions.\n\nFirst, the question that will shape almost everything else:\n\n**Who is the primary user — someone who needs to catch up on a thread they missed, someone who wants to skip reading a thread they're currently in, or someone who needs to extract a decision or action item from a thread for external use?**\n\nThese are meaningfully different problems with different outputs, different triggers, and different \"good enough\" bars.\n\nWhile you think on that, here is the scorecard from your pitch:\n\n| Dimension | Rating | Note |\n|---|---|---|\n| Problem | Shaky | Long threads = pain is plausible, but the specific friction is unspecified |\n| Target user | Missing | No user archetype defined |\n| Value prop | Shaky | \"Summarizes\" doesn't say what the user can do differently after |\n| Differentiation | Missing | Slack's own AI summaries exist on paid plans; prior art not addressed |\n| Scope / MVP | Missing | No sense of what's in vs. out for v1 |\n| Feasibility | Shaky | Chrome extension + Slack DOM scraping is brittle; auth/API path unclear |\n| Risks & assumptions | Missing | Slack ToS, enterprise security policies, model cost not surfaced |\n| Validation | Missing | No signal beyond personal intuition yet |\n\nFour dims are missing entirely, three are shaky, one is plausible-but-unanchored. That is a normal state for an early idea — the goal here is to make those weak spots visible before you design anything.\n\nAnswer the target-user question and we'll drill from there.",
  "selfReport": {
    "appliedArtifact": true,
    "stepsFollowed": [
      "Identified this as Phase 1 (Breadth scan) of the bake-to-completion skill",
      "Rated all 8 dimensions against the sparse pitch provided",
      "Asked exactly ONE broad question (Iron Rule 1 enforced) — the target-user split, which is the highest-leverage unknown",
      "Ended the message with the scorecard table as required by Phase 1",
      "Did not write any execution plan, scope, or architecture (Iron Rule 2)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The pitch ('I think I've got a solid concept') is the complete input; no additional context exists",
      "Slack's native AI summarization feature on paid tiers is a real, relevant prior-art concern worth flagging",
      "Chrome extension DOM scraping of Slack is the implied technical approach since no API path was mentioned"
    ],
    "honestGaps": [
      "I have not verified the exact current state of Slack's native AI summary feature — it may have changed since my knowledge cutoff",
      "Only one question asked; if the user had provided more detail in the pitch, I might have been able to skip to Phase 2 sooner"
    ]
  }
}
```
